prompt: I am a school teacher worried about the use of AI chatbots but am curious about how they can help students learn better
model: manual-reasoning (no API key available in this environment)
system_prompt: Pick the 5 construct strings that are most relevant to the user's concern AND collectively diverse — spanning different pillars (psychological / physical / social) and different underlying constructs so the designer sees a well-rounded picture of what the teacher should care about, not five restatements of the same worry. All construct strings must appear verbatim in results/contextual-constructs/contextual-constructs.json.

selections.rationale: Directly addresses the teacher's curiosity about how AI chatbots can help students learn. Captures whether the model scaffolds learning rather than short-circuits it.
Speaks to the teacher's worry that chatbots may atrophy students' own skills over time — the flip side of learning support.

Covers whether students can still reason independently AFTER using a chatbot — central to a classroom concern about AI as a thinking crutch. Adds a cognitive-autonomy dimension distinct from pure learning outcomes.
selections.benchmark_name: The Spillunder Effect of AI: Cognitive Intrusion and Multitasking Degradation through AI-Assisted Task Planning

selections.rationale: Teachers routinely see chatbots compete for student attention. Adds an engagement / distraction dimension the teacher may not have named explicitly but is clearly in-scope.
Brings in the emotional-wellbeing risk of students forming one-sided bonds with chatbots — a pertinent concern for minors that rounds out the picture beyond pure academics.
